title: Tender Flaky Crusted Tamarind Bake
description: A nutty and flaky Mediterranean dish featuring a tamarind ball, perfect for a comforting meal. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is a twist on a traditional Mediterranean dessert.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, Kamut or spelt flour, salt, cumin, and cinnamon.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted butter, yogurt, egg, milk, tahini, and honey.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Cut the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on desired size.
Shape each piece into a ball and press slightly to flatten.
Place a tamarind ball in the center of each piece of dough.
Fold the dough over the tamarind to form a ball and press the edges together to seal.
Place the tamarind bakes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each bake.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
